decisions.what: OTLP telemetry routes remain GET consumer-pull only; POST ingest not added
decisions.why: The daemon is a telemetry producer, not a collector. GET endpoints export recorded telemetry to external collectors. Adding POST OTLP ingest would make the daemon a collector, conflating two distinct roles. Operators who need push-over-HTTP delivery should configure an external OTLP collector (e.g. OpenTelemetry Collector) to receive from the daemon.

decisions.what: Companion token store moved from .goodvibes/<surface>/companion-token.json to .goodvibes/operator-tokens.json
decisions.why: Per-surface token paths mean TUI-embedded and standalone daemon postures each generate separate tokens. A companion app paired with TUI cannot connect to the standalone daemon (different token, different surface path). Moving to a single workspace-level path ensures one token works across all postures.
decisions.migration: Existing companions must re-pair after upgrade. Old token files at .goodvibes/tui/companion-token.json and .goodvibes/daemon/companion-token.json are no longer read.

decisions.what: dispatchDaemonApiRoutes extended with optional DaemonApiRouteExtension[] parameter
decisions.why: Companion-chat and provider routes use SDK-internal context types (CompanionChatRouteContext, ProviderRegistry) that daemon-sdk cannot depend on directly. Rather than copying or re-exporting internal types, we expose an extension slot that lets standalone daemon binaries inject these route dispatchers at composition time without modifying core files.

decisions.what: Voice TTS/STT/realtime errors for unconfigured providers now return 409 PROVIDER_NOT_CONFIGURED instead of 404
decisions.why: 404 implies the route does not exist. 409 Conflict conveys that the resource (voice provider configuration) is in a state that prevents the request from proceeding, matching the HTTP semantics for a missing configuration dependency.

decisions.what: Agent spawn capacity exceeded now returns 429 with Retry-After: 5 instead of 500
decisions.why: HTTP 500 is for server errors; capacity throttling is an expected backpressure condition, not a bug. 429 Too Many Requests is the correct semantic and enables clients to implement automatic retry with backoff.

decisions.what: Identity files (auth-users.json, auth-bootstrap.txt, operator-tokens.json) relocated to daemonHomeDir (~/.goodvibes/daemon/ default), separate from runtime workingDir
decisions.why: Workspace swaps must not invalidate companion pairings or authentication state. Mixing identity files in the workspace directory meant every workspace swap could orphan paired companions. Separating identity (stable, tied to the machine/operator) from workspace (mutable, the active project directory) makes swaps safe.

decisions.what: WorkspaceSwapManager refuses swap with WORKSPACE_BUSY (409) when any session has pendingInputCount > 0
decisions.why: Swapping while a session is actively processing input could corrupt in-flight context or lose messages. The retryAfter: 5 seconds gives callers a concrete backoff target. Sessions with no pending input (idle) are safe to move across store re-roots.

decisions.what: F3 revision: operator-tokens.json moved from workspace-scoped path to daemonHomeDir
decisions.why: 0.21.17 moved tokens to <cwd>/.goodvibes/operator-tokens.json for workspace portability, but this introduced a new problem: workspace swaps would change the working directory and thus invalidate the token path. Moving to daemon home ensures the token store survives workspace swaps.

decisions.what: GOODVIBES_CHAT_LIMITER_THRESHOLD env var overrides per-session companion chat rate limit; precedence: explicit config > env > default
decisions.why: Operators running automated tests or high-throughput pipelines need to tune the rate limit without code changes. The env var enables runtime configuration. The explicit config option always wins so library users can lock the limit programmatically.

decisions.what: Pre-existing blocker framing is prohibited in this project
decisions.why: User memory directive: 'Nothing is pre-existing — if a bug/failure exists in HEAD, it gets fixed; never label as pre-existing or out of scope.' Reviewers and agents must not use the framing 'pre-existing' to defer or dismiss any bug or failure present in the working tree.

decisions.what: Flag-gated feature knobs promoted to live CONFIG_SCHEMA keys, plus a FEATURE_FLAG_CONFIG grouping map (flag -> config categories/keys) covering all 44 flags
decisions.why: Owner ruling: every feature's meaningful sub-options must be settable through the interactive config surface, not left behind a bare flag toggle. Pattern: (1) add scalar keys to schema-types.ts ConfigKey union + ConfigValue map (unavoidable there); new top-level categories use a `declare module './schema-types.js'` GoodVibesConfig augmentation in a domain file (schema-domain-features.ts) like the worktree idiom, so schema-types.ts interface stays put; (2) defaults equal prior hardcoded values; (3) wire a genuine READ at the best in-scope site — runtime/services.ts composition root has configManager (overflow, token-audit, provider-optimizer, policy bundle load); factories thread an optional configManager (phased-executor budget, notification router burst, permission simulator, delivery queue); the agent orchestrators read via their in-scope configManager (passive-injection budget/floor/codeLimit, context-compact threshold). Constructor/per-call params stay as override seams (config is the DEFAULT source). FEATURE_FLAG_CONFIG.configKeys is typed ConfigKey[] so a bad key fails typecheck; flag-config-map.test.ts guards bidirectional completeness. httpListener CORS was wired to the existing controlPlane.cors.* keys, not a parallel surface.

decisions.what: packages/sdk/package.json's optionalDependencies.openai changed from a caret range ("^6.29.0") to an exact pin ("6.46.0")
decisions.why: 1.7.0 shipped with openai resolving via caret range. bun.lock pinned openai@6.35.0, so `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in CI always tested the safe older resolution — but a fresh consumer install (npm install / bun add with no matching lockfile) followed the semver range to whatever was newest (6.46.0), which broke: openai's client constructor started throwing on a falsy apiKey somewhere between 6.29 and 6.46 (previously only threw on `undefined`), crashing every discovered/anonymous local provider (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, TGI, LocalAI) at registration. Root cause class: the lockfile shielded CI from exactly what fresh installs got. Fixed for 1.7.1 in two parts: (1) substitute a harmless placeholder apiKey ('gv-local', matching the pre-existing builtin-registry.ts anonymous-provider convention) at every `new OpenAI(...)` construction site (openai-compat.ts, openai.ts, lm-studio-helpers.ts) whenever the effective key is empty, deriving configured/isConfigured() status from the ORIGINAL apiKey; (2) exact-pin openai itself, matching this SAME dependency block's existing exact-pin precedent on cloudflare/google-auth-library/pdfjs-dist. Any other optionalDependency still on a caret range carries the same latent risk class (CI-green-but-fresh-install-broken) and should be reviewed the same way if it starts causing consumer-reported breaks.

decisions.what: Providers must declare modelSource ('live-discovery' | 'dated-static' + asOf | 'catalog-backed') when their own models array is empty at registration; ProviderRegistry.register()/registerRuntimeProvider() throw otherwise. Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini get real live discovery (GET /v1/models, ListModels) with dated-static baselines populated synchronously at construction; OpenAI-codex/github-copilot/bedrock/bedrock-mantle/vertex stay dated-static only (no listing API). Live/dated-static results feed a new providerNativeModels bucket in the model registry (registry-live-model-discovery.ts), merged ABOVE the third-party models.dev catalog for the same registryKey (provider truth beats a possibly-stale aggregator) but only filling gaps the catalog doesn't already cover richer.
decisions.why: Root cause: AnthropicProvider/OpenAIProvider/OpenAICodexProvider had `readonly models: string[] = []` with the ONLY real model source being the third-party models.dev catalog fetch — meaning a brand-new model (e.g. claude-fable-5, claude-sonnet-5, verified live against a real Anthropic API key 2026-07-12) was unselectable until that third-party snapshot caught up, with no structural guard against a provider shipping a permanently-empty, undated array. Making refreshModels()'s dated-static baseline apply SYNCHRONOUSLY at construction (not just after an async fetch resolves) means a fresh boot with no network is never worse than before.

decisions.what: Live-model-discovery's background sweep is a SEPARATE method (ProviderRegistry.initProviderModelDiscovery()) from initCatalog(), not bundled into it, even though both are 'background, TTL-respecting, called once at startup' in spirit.
decisions.why: initCatalog() has zero callers inside this repo (only the models.dev third-party catalog fetch it does); it's called by external consumers (TUI/agent daemons) AND by many of this repo's own tests directly. Bundling the new live-discovery sweep into it would have made every such test silently fire a real network call to Anthropic's live API using whatever ANTHROPIC_API_KEY happens to be in the test-runner's environment (fire-and-forget, wouldn't fail tests, but is genuinely undesirable test hygiene and an unrequested real API call). Consumers should call both methods at startup; this repo's own composition root (runtime/services.ts) now calls initProviderModelDiscovery() right after initCustomProviders().

decisions.what: One shared resolver (providers/model-id-resolution.ts, resolveModelReference) implements: already-qualified passes through unchanged; provider-in-context qualifies; unique-across-registry auto-qualifies; ambiguous throws listing real candidate registryKeys; unknown throws with Levenshtein closest-match suggestions + one concrete valid example from the live registry. Wired directly (no opt-out) into ProviderRegistry.getForModel/setCurrentModel/getCapabilityForModel/explainRoute and AgentOrchestrator.resolveProviderRouting since those always have real registry access. Wired as an OPTIONAL parameter (models/modelCandidates/providerRegistry) into automation (buildDefaultExecution etc.), control-plane/session-intents.ts (buildSharedSessionAgentSpawnRoutingInput), and tools/agent/manager.ts (AgentManager.spawn) — omitted callers keep the exact prior format-only validation so existing tests/callers that haven't threaded the registry through are unaffected.
decisions.why: The prework site map (prework-bare-model-id-resolution.md) separated 'registry access already in scope' sites from 'needs a registry dependency threaded in' sites specifically because the latter group (AgentManager had ZERO registry field; AutomationManager/session-intents had no registry dependency at all) could not safely be made to always resolve without breaking every existing caller/test that constructs them without a registry. The optional-parameter pattern was chosen over a required constructor dependency to avoid a breaking change to every construction site across the whole SDK+consumers in one round; the real composition root (runtime/services.ts, daemon/facade.ts) DOES wire the real ProviderRegistry through now, so production behavior is the full resolver everywhere it matters.
